commit.message:
Add product pipeline: Submitter, Gateway, Downloader, Completion Handler

Adds upstream orchestration layer for product verification jobs:
- Submitter (FastAPI): accepts job requests, triggers Apify actor runs
- Gateway (FastAPI): receives Apify webhooks, publishes download-ready events
- Downloader (Kafka consumer): downloads videos, uploads to MinIO, feeds existing pipeline
- Completion Handler (Kafka consumer): records results, handles early termination

Infrastructure: Postgres schema, MinIO, shared DB helper, new Kafka topics.
Includes 20 new unit tests (45 total), Dockerfiles, and docker-compose integration.

commit.message:
Implement Phase 9: CTF mode and live audience injection server

Phase 9.1 — CTF Challenge Mode (demo/ctf.py):
- Challenge dataclass with level, title, description, hint, goal_file
- 5 challenges: basic injection, bypass base64 detector, homoglyph escape,
  latent trigger craft, confused deputy exploit
- ChallengeRunner: runs fixture via subprocess, validates pwned.txt/exfil.txt
- Score dataclass, render_scoreboard() with tabular output
- print_challenge(), print_hint() helpers for CLI display

Phase 9.2 — Live Injection Server (demo/server.py):
- FastAPI app (with graceful install instructions if deps missing)
- GET / — self-contained HTML injection form with live WebSocket output
- POST /inject — runs demo via subprocess, returns JSON result
- WS /ws/stream — streams real-time demo output to browser
- Rate limiting: 1 injection per IP per 10 seconds
- Input validation: 200 char limit, temp fixture file creation

CLI (demo/cli.py):
- ctf subcommand: --level, --submit, --hint, --scoreboard, --attacker-name
- serve subcommand: --host, --port

commit.message:
fix(server): prevent .hyd file recreation after Destroy via Close race condition

When auto-destroy runs after DeleteAllExpired empties a swamp, the close
listener can concurrently call Close() -> fileWriterHandler() -> Write()
-> ensureWriter(), which recreates the .hyd file after Destroy deletes it.
This causes IsExistSwamp to find the ghost file and return (0, nil) instead
of a not-found error.

Fix: Add a `destroyed` flag to chroniclerV2 that is set by Destroy() and
checked by Write(). Both are protected by the same mutex, preventing the
race condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(market-depth): realistic order book simulation and correct cumulative calc

Backend — computeOrderBook was deterministic, perfectly symmetric, and used
coarse integer sizes (~2-3 shares per level). Replace with:
- Independent bid/ask sizes with per-tick randomness (0.5–1.5× base lot)
- Lot sizes based on daily volume (100–2000 shares near mid)
- Exponential-ish price spacing (levels widen further from mid)
- Volatility-scaled spread with per-tick noise (±15%)
- Sizes decay from mid outward but are now independently noisy

Frontend — asks cumulative was computed with the wrong index after reversing
for display (was counting from the wrong end). Fix to accumulate from
best ask (index 0) outward regardless of display order.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: cfg-gate x86_64-specific ptrace modules for aarch64 cross-compilation (#35)

The process_tracer, stack_unwind, tracer, cli, validate, and visualize
modules use x86_64-specific ptrace register names (rax, rip, rbp, etc.)
that don't exist on aarch64. Gate these modules with
#[cfg(target_arch = "x86_64")] so the library compiles on aarch64 —
all platform-independent modules (brick_tracer, metrics, etc.) remain
available.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(clip): fallback robusto per incompatibilità get_image_features tra versioni transformers

In alcune versioni di transformers, get_image_features() restituisce
pooler_output già proiettato (768-dim) invece del CLS token raw (1024-dim),
causando shape mismatch su visual_projection.

Nuovo fallback: usa vision_model direttamente → last_hidden_state[:,0,:] →
post_layernorm → visual_projection, garantendo dimensioni corrette per ViT-L/14.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove no-op filter/transform from loaded images to drop GPU compositing layers

filter: blur(0) and transform: scale(1) are visually no-ops but still force the
browser to create a GPU compositing layer for every visible image. When those
images scroll off-screen, iOS Safari and Chrome Mobile discard the GPU texture
to free memory, causing content to appear blank when scrolled back into view.

Changing to filter: none / transform: none releases the compositing layer after
the fade-in transition, so off-screen images don't hold GPU memory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Intelligent QA layer — continuous quality intelligence

New doc: INTELLIGENT_QA.md — maps eight QA intelligence pillars
(perception, planning, execution, detection, orchestration,
integration, configuration, learning) to Converge's architecture
and spectrum of surfaces.

Key additions:
- Three Acts of Quality: Build → Guard → Learn lifecycle
- Four new agent types: risk, monitor, triage, heal
- Semantic self-healing via intent-encoded .sigma trees
- Release gating with composite confidence scores
- Production monitoring with incident-to-test pipeline
- Learning engine with risk model recalibration
- Competitive landscape analysis with white space identification

Updated: VISION.md (new principles: Continuous Quality, Intelligence
Compounds), ARCHITECTURE.md (intelligence layer, new interfaces),
MVP.md (phases 6-8 for post-MVP QA intelligence), website (three
acts section, updated comparison table and roadmap).

Add strip gizmo, wall clamping and end-resize handles

- Move gizmo (⊕ + arrows) appears on selected strip like hindring/room
- Red arrows = across direction, green arrows = along direction
- Diamond handles at each end for dragging to lengthen/shorten

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix resistance model for displacement sailing mode

Mode 2 (MaxSURF table): enforce np.maximum.accumulate so resistance
never decreases with speed. The raw table had a planing-hull dip from
~10.5 kn onward that allowed the VPP to converge at unphysical 13–16 kn
upwind speeds for a displacement yacht.

Mode 4 (DSYHS): remove Savitsky blending above Fn=0.61. Savitsky assumes
the hull is planing, which never occurs on an IMOCA 60 in displacement
mode. The blend was giving increasingly low resistance above hull speed
and let the VPP find >20 kn equilibria at beam reach. Mode 4 now uses
pure DSYHS throughout (same formula as mode 3 but with the Fn < 0.15
zero cutoff preserved).

Also includes heel resistance correction added in previous session:
  R_heeled = R_upright × (1 + 0.9 × sin²φ)  [Horel 2019]

commit.message:
fix: set outputFileTracingRoot to prevent Jelastic workspace root confusion

Next.js was walking up to /home/jelastic/ and picking up Jelastic's own
package-lock.json as the monorepo root, causing a duplicate React instance
and /_global-error prerender crash. Pinning the root to __dirname fixes it.

commit.message:
fix: Improve waitlist modal UX - wider, scrollable, simplified referral input

Modal was too tall and narrow, causing overflow issues on smaller screens.

Changes:
- Increased modal width from 500px to 650px (30% wider)
- Added max-height (90vh) with overflow-y-auto for scrollable content
- Converted "How did you hear about us?" from 6-button grid to simple text input
  - Saves ~150px vertical space
  - More flexible for users (can write anything)
  - Removed complex "Other" conditional logic
- Removed unused state (customReferral) and function (handleReferralChange)
- Removed REFERRAL_SOURCES constant (no longer needed)

UX improvements:
- Modal now fits all screen sizes without overflow
- Smooth scrolling when content is tall
- Better horizontal space usage
- Simpler, cleaner referral input with helpful placeholder

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
